name: Innocence Project
mission: By entering your email address, you agree to receive emails from the Innocence Project. By entering your phone number, you agree to receive recurring automated promotional and personalized marketing text messages (e.g. cart reminders) from The Innocence Project at the cell number used when signing up. Consent is not a condition of any purchase. Reply HELP for help and STOP to cancel. Msg frequency varies. Msg & data rates may apply. View Terms & Privacy.
theoryOfChange: The Innocence Project has published a strategic framework titled 'In the Vanguard of Justice Reform: The Road Ahead'. This document outlines a new set of priorities for the organization, structured around three foundational pillars: Restoring Freedom, Transforming Systems, and Advancing the Movement. It details how the Innocence Project plans to expand exoneration efforts, strengthen scientific initiatives, and challenge racial bias in wrongful conviction cases. Additionally, the 'Transforming Systems' page on their website further describes their approach to eliminating flaws within the criminal legal system through legislative changes, strengthening police and prosecutorial accountability, and improving standards for forensic science.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial health and transparency, balanced by the high costs associated with systemic legal reform and advocacy work.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary due to the intensive nature of legal advocacy
Limited alignment with specific donor demographics seeking direct service over systemic advocacy

dimension_explanations.alignment: The project is classified as sadaqah-eligible for those interested in justice and advocacy, though it has a low donor fit for those seeking traditional religious charitable frameworks [7]. Its work addresses the urgent cause of criminal justice reform through a proven track record of intake reviews and legal victories [3][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on systemic change through intake evaluations and full exonerations, though the cost per beneficiary is high at $86,746.42 [4][6]. With a program expense ratio of 74.6%, the majority of its $33,643,969 revenue is directed toward litigation and policy reform [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Innocence Project Inc works to exonerate the wrongly convicted and reform the criminal justice system through post-conviction litigation and policy advocacy.

Exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.10 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 41.7 months of working capital

strengths_deep_dive: The Innocence Project's strength lies in its dual-track approach of direct legal intervention and systemic advocacy. By 2017, they had completed <cite id="13">2,000 intake evaluations</cite>, showcasing a rigorous pipeline for case selection. This work is supported by a highly efficient fundraising machine that spends only <cite id="5">$0.11 to raise every dollar</cite>, far outperforming industry benchmarks. Furthermore, their governance is bolstered by a <cite id="14">board of 17 members</cite> providing diverse oversight of their <cite id="15">$105.8 million in total assets</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Innocence Project Inc is a leading national nonprofit <cite id="1">founded in 1992</cite> that works to exonerate the wrongly convicted through DNA testing and criminal justice reform. The organization maintains a <cite id="2">perfect 100/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting its commitment to accountability and leadership. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $32,455,846</cite>, with a significant portion derived from <cite id="4">$26,644,804 in public contributions</cite>. Their operational model is highly efficient, maintaining a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.10 per $1 raised</cite>. Beyond individual litigation, the Project focuses on <cite id="6">systemic policy reform and strategic litigation</cite> to address the root causes of wrongful convictions. Historically, the organization has achieved significant milestones, including reaching <cite id="7">200 total exonerations by 2017</cite> and increasing that figure to <cite id="8">220 by 2019</cite>. Their legal intake process is extensive, having <cite id="9">reviewed 1,806 intake letters</cite> in a single recent reporting period to identify potential cases of innocence.
